governance,leadership	Cuba
President
The president of Cuba made a public statement that influenced the escalation of protests.

arson,attack	Communist Party of Cuba
Protesters
Protesters in some cities set fire to local offices of the Communist Party of Cuba.

authorization,logistics	Russia
U.S. Coast Guard
U.S. Coast Guard permitted a Russian oil tanker to proceed to Cuba despite the U.S. oil blockade.

reporting,source	New York Times
U.S. Coast Guard
New York Times reported that the U.S. Coast Guard authorized the Russian oil tanker to reach Cuba.

non-benefit,oil trade	Russia
United States
Donald Trump stated that the United States does not object to Russian oil being delivered to Cuba but emphasized that Russia would not profit from it.

oil shipment	Cuba
Russia
Russia is supplying oil to Cuba via a tanker authorized by the U.S. Coast Guard.

sanction,trade prohibition	Cuba
U.S. Department of the Treasury
U.S. Department of the Treasury added Cuba to a list of countries banned from purchasing Russian oil.

dependency,energy crisis	Cuba
oil shipment
Cuba is dependent on oil shipments, and the blockade has previously caused total blackouts on the island.

executive action,presidency,statement	Donald Trump
United States
Donald Trump, as President of the United States, made public statements regarding U.S. policy on oil deliveries to Cuba.<SEP>Donald Trump, as President of the United States, signed an executive order on behalf of the United States government to impose tariffs.

official communication,source	Donald Trump
White House
The White House is the official source of the statement justifying the tariffs, issued under the authority of Donald Trump.

media coverage,reporting	Ivan Dyakonov
United States
Ivan Dyakonov authored an article reporting on the U.S. policy decision to impose tariffs related to Cuba.

economic sanction,national security policy	Cuba
Donald Trump
Donald Trump signed an order targeting Cuba by imposing tariffs on nations that supply it with oil, directly linking the policy to Cuba's regime.

media coverage,source attribution	Ivan Dyakonov
White House
Ivan Dyakonov's article cites the White House as the source of the official statement regarding the tariff policy.

blockade,conditional proposal,diplomatic engagement,economic sanction,national security policy,negotiation,secret negotiation,trade restriction	Cuba
United States
The president of Cuba announced the beginning of negotiations with the United States.<SEP>United States has imposed an oil blockade on Cuba since January and prohibited Cuba from purchasing Russian oil.<SEP>The United States imposed tariffs on countries supplying oil to Cuba, framing the action as a measure to counter Cuba's threat to U.S. national security.<SEP>The United States conducted secret negotiations with Cuba in Havana, proposing the lifting of trade embargo and access to Starlink in exchange for democratic reforms, release of political prisoners, and compensation for assets seized after the 1959 Revolution.

proposal,technology provision	Starlink
United States
The United States proposed providing access to Starlink satellite communication to Cuba as part of a conditional offer during negotiations.

demand,diplomatic meeting	Cuba
State Department
The State Department of the United States met with Cuban authorities in Havana, demanding the release of political prisoners and compensation for confiscated assets, and expressing concern over foreign military and intelligence activities near U.S. territory.

asset confiscation,historical grievance	1959 Revolution
United States
The United States demanded compensation for American citizens and corporations whose assets were confiscated following the 1959 Revolution in Cuba.

leadership connection,political representation	Cuba
Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro
Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro, grandson of Raúl Castro, represented Cuban leadership in secret talks with the United States, indicating high-level involvement in negotiations.

diplomatic precedent	Barack Obama
United States
The 2026 secret talks between the United States and Cuba marked the first direct engagement since President Barack Obama’s visit to Cuba in 2016, establishing a diplomatic precedent.

Julius Malema is the leader of the EFF, a South African radical left-wing political party.

Julius Malema sang the chant "Kill the Boer," which targets the Boer population as symbols of colonial oppression, inciting racial hostility.

In Sudáfrica, the term "Boer" historically refers to white Afrikaans-speaking descendants, primarily farmers, who are central to the colonial and post-colonial conflict.
